Module 1 · Lesson 5
The Six Teams — Who Does What at NorthBridge
Click any circle to explore each team's role in the Monday morning incident
⚡ Key Principle: Every alert, outage, and deployment at NorthBridge passes through at least one of these six teams. Knowing who owns what is the first step to understanding how real operations work.
1
Setting the Scene
NorthBridge at Night
The ops floor before the team arrives — and the screen that was already awake
2
SOC
Security Operations Centre
Monitors every login, alert, and unusual pattern — 24 × 7
3
SRE
Site Reliability Engineering
Owns system health, latency, and error-rate SLAs for every service
4
NOC
Network Operations Centre
Watches every switch, router, and link — the first to see a node go dark
5
DevOps
The Deployment Team
Owns the CI/CD pipeline — and the deployment that went to production at 14:08
6
IT Ops · Platform Eng
IT Operations & Platform Engineering
Physical racks, cabling, and cloud infrastructure — quiet competence on a normal Tuesday
7
All Six Teams
Monday Morning — Mapped
Every team, every layer — who was watching what when the incident began
8
Infrastructure
The Servers They Protect
The physical foundation — rows of servers running every NorthBridge service
9
Ready for Monday
The Full Floor at 09:00
All dashboards green, all monitors on — and the clock just hit nine
SOC — Security
SRE — Reliability
NOC — Network
DevOps — Deployment
IT Ops — Physical
Platform Eng — Cloud
